title: First Interest Payment — Amortizing the Discount
event: Continuing Reyes Co.'s discount bonds: $500,000 face, 8% stated, issued at $461,391 to yield 10% (semiannual periods; discount $38,609). Prepare the first semiannual interest entry.

lines.why: Cash coupon plus an equal slice of the discount each period.
lines.trap: Straight-line expense is CONSTANT — the tell that distinguishes it from effective interest.

lines.label: 38,609 ÷ 10
lines.why: One-tenth of the discount amortized per period.
lines.trap: The contra shrinks toward zero, walking carrying value up to face by maturity.

lines.label: 500,000 × 8% ÷ 2
lines.why: The coupon is fixed by the STATED rate — same check every period, either method.
lines.trap: Cash never changes with the method — only the expense and amortization split does.

lines.label: 461,391 × 5%
lines.why: Market rate per period times CARRYING VALUE — the economically true cost.
lines.trap: Not 5% × face (25,000) and not 4% × carrying value — market rate on carrying value, always.

lines.label: 23,070 − 20,000
lines.why: The gap between true expense and cash paid amortizes the discount.
lines.trap: Under effective interest the amortization GROWS each period as carrying value climbs.

lines.label: 500,000 × 8% ÷ 2
lines.trap: New carrying value: 461,391 + 3,070 = 464,461 — next period's expense base.

memorize.body: Effective interest: expense = market rate/period × carrying value; amortization = expense − cash coupon. Straight-line: amortization = discount ÷ number of periods.
Either way, cash = face × stated ÷ 2 and carrying value marches toward face — the methods only disagree on the path.

questions.prompt: Effective interest method: carrying value $461,391, market 10% annual. First-period interest EXPENSE = ?

distractors.feedback: You applied 5% to the 500,000 FACE. Effective interest runs on CARRYING VALUE: 5% × 461,391.
You applied the stated 4% to carrying value. The stated rate writes checks; the MARKET rate measures expense.
That's the cash coupon. Expense exceeds cash on discount bonds — the 3,070 gap amortizes the discount.

build.scaffold: Anchor on cash (face × stated ÷ 2), compute expense by the method, and the discount line is the difference.
